Press enter or click to view image in full size

A few days ago I published Lynnix to npm. Today the documentation site is live at lynnix.vercel.app/docs.
Lynnix is a file-based routing and SSR middleware for Node.js. It is designed specifically for HTMX applications — the kind where the server produces HTML and HTMX handles interactivity on the client. You create a directory, drop files into it following a set of naming conventions, and Lynnix handles routing, rendering, middleware chains, HTMX-aware partial responses, and error boundaries automatically.
No config file. No build step. Just a filesystem that speaks HTTP.
The docs cover 14 pages: introduction, installation, getting started, project structure, file conventions, routing, loaders, middleware, layouts, fragments and HTMX, error handling, the request API, the response API, and framework integration.
The site itself is built on Lynnix and rendered with Mutor.js — the template engine that powers Lynnix’s rendering layer.
The documentation is not wholesome and will be improved over time, but it’s good enough to start with.
https://lynnix.vercel.app/docs
https://github.com/allAboutJS/Lynnix/activity
Leave a review (and a star 😊).
Thank you.
